Facts.text: Second and final revision of the Justinian Code is published, a codified set of imperial and classical laws initially begun in 528
The case of the 'Holy Child of La Guardia', involving the kidnapping and murdering a Christian boy, concludes with public burning at the stake of nine Jews in Ávila, Spain
City of Havana moved to its current location to avoid mosquito infestations
Troops under Spanish General Fadrique Alvarez de Toledo occupy and plunder Zutphen, Netherlands
First colonial prison organized in Nantucket, Massachusetts
French troops occupy Freiburg

Holy Roman Emperor Leopold I and Elector of Brandenburg Frederick I sign the Crown Treaty, allowing Prussia to be elevated to a kingdom in exchange for 8,000 troops in the upcoming War of the Spanish Succession
Native Americans surrender to the British during Pontiac's War of Odawa Chief Pontiac
Dutch West India Company and Amsterdam divide Suriname between themselves
1st gun salute for an American warship in a foreign port - US Andrew Doria at Fort St Eustatius (Dutch Caribbean isalnd)
American Revolutionary War: British and Hessian troops take Fort Washington, New York marking one of biggest losses by US forces
Kentucky becomes first state to nullify an act of Congress
First edition of New York Evening Post
Battle at Schongrabern: Russian army stop French
New York City's Fifth Avenue opens for business
New Zealand officially becomes a British colony
Life preservers made of cork are patented by Napoleon Guerin (NYC)
Amsterdam post office at Nieuwezijds Voorburgwal opens
Battle of Campbell's Station TN, 492 causalities
Confederate retreat at Lovejoy, Georgia
Spanish Parliament, "the Cortes" formally elects Italian Prince Amedeo Ferdinando Maria as King Amadeo I of Spain
Battle of Gundet: Ethiopian emperor Yohannes beats Egyptians
William Bonwill, patents dental mallet to impact gold into cavities
British gunboat HMS Flirt fires at and destroys villages of Abari and Asaba on the Forcado River in Niger, in retaliation for attack on British owned factory that left 5 Brits missing
6,000 Armenians massacred by Turks in Kurdistan
French captain Henri Decoeurs troops reach Nikki, West Africa
3 autos race on Ocean Parkway, Brooklyn, fastest speed achieved by Henry Fournier who drives a mile in 51 4/5 seconds
V Herbert & H Smith's musical "Babette" premieres in NYC
Dutch Russian Count Witte becomes Prime Minister of Russia
Oklahoma becomes the United States 46th state
Dutch football club FC Eindhoven is founded in southern part of the city; Eredivisie 1954; KNVB Cup 1937
Federal Reserve System formally opens
I. Berlin, V. Herbert, H. Blossoms musical premieres in NYC
Russian La Satannaya ammunition factory explodes, killing 1,000
British occupy Tel Aviv and Jaffa
Hungarian People's Republic declared
Admiral Miklós Horthy, head of the Hungarian National Army, seizes Budapest and will later become regent of the restored Kingdom of Hungary
1st postage stamp meter is set in Stamford Conn
Australia's Qantas airways founded in Winton, Queensland as Queensland and Northern Territory Aerial Services Limited
Cleveland Bulldogs lose, 12-7 to Frankford Yellow Jackets at Dunn Field; ends 31-game undefeated streak; NFL and major-league football record
American Association for Advancement of Atheism forms (NY)
Philip Barry's play "In a Garden" premieres in NYC
New York Rangers ice hockey club first game; beat Montreal Maroons, 1-0 at Madison Square Garden, NYC
Swiss physicist Fritz Zwicky publishes the first evidence for the existence of dark matter in his seminal article "The Redshift of Extragalactic Nebulae"
German air force begins bombing of Madrid

Several members of the Hesse-Darmstadt royal family die in a plane crash in Belgium while enroute to a family wedding in England; mother and brother of the groom among the dead as aircraft clipped a tall factory chimney
K B Regiment refuses round-table conference in East-India
German U-boat torpedoes tanker Sliedrecht near Ireland
World War II: In response to Germany's leveling of Coventry, England two days before, the Royal Air Force bombs Hamburg.
German troops conquer Kertsh (probably)
Assault of US B-17 Flying Fortresses on airport at Sidi Ahmed
WW II: American bombers strike a hydro-electric power facility and heavy water factory in German-controlled Vemork, Norway
US 9th division & 1st Army attacks at Geilenkirchen
Founding of UNESCO, the United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization
Two new elements discovered by Glenn Seaborg, James, Morgan and Albert Ghiorso were are announced: americium (atomic number 95) and curium (atomic number 96)
Yeshiva College (University), chartered in NY, 1st US Jewish College
15,000 demonstrate in Brussels against mild sentence of Nazis
Egyptian King Faruk demands departure of all British troops
UN gets US government approval to issue postage stamps
US President Harry Truman proclaims emergency crisis caused by communist threat
Papagos' Greek Concentratie wins Greeks parliamentary election
Sultan Sidi Mohammed Ben Yussuph V returns to Morocco
Guido Cantelli, Italian conductor, named Musical Director of La Scala Teatro in Milan, Italy
University of Oklahoma Football NCAA win streak ends at 47 after losing 7-0 to Notre Dame at Oklahoma Memorial Stadium
Boston business executive Billy Sullivan is awarded eighth and final franchise of developing American Football League (AFL); later branded Boston Patriots
Musical "The Sound of Music" byRichard Rodgersand Oscar Hammerstein II opens at the Lunt-Fontanne Theatre in NYC; runs for 1,443 performances
NL batting champion Dick Groat of the Pittsburgh Pirateds wins MVP
United Kingdom limits immigration from Commonwealth countries
Kuwait adopts constitution (1st, Islamitic)
Toledo, OH newspaper strike began
Radio CJCX Sydney Nova Scotia (Canada) starts shortwave transmission
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
Venera 3 launched, 1st to land on another planet (crashes into Venus)
Walt Disney launches Epcot Center: Experimental Prototype Community of Tomorrow
"Greatest Hits" album by The Temptations is released (Billboard Album of the Year 1967)
Dr Sam Sheppard freed by a jury after 9 years in jail
Ken Loach's television play "Cathy Come Home" is broadcast on the BBC, challenging attitudes towards homelessness in Britain
The Derry Citizens Action Committee defies a ban on marches in Derry, Northern Ireland, by marching with an estimated 15,000 people
1968 Mỹ Lai massacre of between 347 and 504 unarmed South Vietnamese civilians by US soldiers is first reported
South Vietnamese Vice President Nguyen Cao Ky defends operations in Cambodia because communist forces could overrun South Vietnam "within 24 hours" if troops operating there were withdrawn
Two men are shot dead by the Irish Republican Army (IRA)
The Compton inquiry is published, acknowledging that there was ill-treatment of internees, but rejected claims of systematic brutality or torture (Northern Ireland)
The US increase air activity to support the Cambodian government as fighting neared Phnom Penh

Elektra Records releases "No Secrets', the third studio album by American singer-songwriter Carly Simon; her commercial breakthrough spends 5 weeks at top of US charts and contains the hit singles "You're So Vain" and "The Right Thing to Do"
Skylab 4 launched into Earth orbit
US PresidentRichard Nixonauthorizes construction of the Alaskan pipeline
1st intentional interstellar radio message sent from Arecibo Observatory's radio telescope in Puerto Rico towards M 13, a cluster of stars some 25,000 light years away
John Lennon's single "Whatever Gets You Through the Night", featuring Elton John, goes to #1 in US; the last of the Beatles with a solo chart-topper, it was the only one during his lifetime
NBA Milwaukee Bucks lose their  team record 11th straight game, fallimg 92-89 to vhe visiting Cleveland Cavaliers
René Levesque's Parti Québécois wins elections in Quebec
Major Indoor Soccer League holds its 1st draft
Tampa Bay Buccaneer QB Doug Williams throws for 486 yards
Dennis Lillee kicksJaved Miandadafter he had waved his bat at Dennis
5th NASA Space Shuttle Mission Columbia lands at Edwards Air Force Base after 5 days in space, completing its 1st operational flight
Agreement reached ending 57 day football strike
14th Shuttle Mission (51A) -Discovery 2- lands at Kennedy Space Center (Florida)
Houston blocks 20 Denver shots tying NBA regulation game record
John Lennon's 'Every Man has a Woman Who Loves Him' released posthumously
Paul McCartneyreleases single "Once Upon a Long Ago"
Estonia declares sovereignty in internal affairs
President Reaganmeets with Jerusalem's mayor Teddy Kollek
Six Jesuit priests including Ignacio Ellacuría and two others are assassinated killed by Salvadoran army - one of the most notorious acts of the Salvadoran civil war
UNESCO adopts the Seville Statement on Violence at the 25th session of its General Conference, stating "we are not condemned to war and violence because of our biology"
US Attorney General Janet Reno announces she has Parkinson's disease
People's Republic of China releases pro-democracy dissident Wei Jingsheng from jail for medical reasons after nearly 18 years of incarceration
Revival of Sherman Edwards' historical musical "1776", featuring Brent Spiner, closes at the Criterion Theater to transfer to the George Gershwin Theatre, NYC
"Let Me Love You" single released by Mario (Billboard Song of the Year 2005)
'Call of Duty: Black Ops 2' grosses $500 million in 24 hours to become the biggest entertainment launch of all time
Klaus Iohannis wins the Romanian Presidential election
Novak Đokovićclaims 3 straight ATP World Tour Finals tennis titles after Roger Federer is forced to withdraw from the final in London through injury
Largest diamond discovered in more than a century, a 1,111 carat stone found in the Karowe mine, Botswana
19 countries pledge to phase out coal at UN Climate Summit in Bonn, Germany
Cambodian court rules to dissolve the country's main opposition party Cambodia National Rescue party (CNRP)
US senator Al Franken accused of groping and forcibly kissing a woman
Elevator falls down 84 floors when hoist rope breaks, all six people survive unharmed at John Hancock Center, Chicago
The kilogram is refined by abstract constants replacing the Le Grand K, along with the ampere (electrical current) and kelvin (temperature) at a conference in Paris
500th anniversary of the founding of Havana, Cuba
Britain's Prince Andrew refutes claims he had sex with 17 year-old connected to sex offender and friend Jeffery Epstein in BBC interview
Samoa declares state of emergency over measles epidemic closing all schools after six deaths
Sri Lankan Presidential election, won by former defense secretary Gotabaya Rajapaksa
Hurricane Iota makes landfall in Nicaragua as a category four storm, just 15 km south of Hurricane Eta 13 days ago
Peru's Congress votes in its third interim President in a week, Francisco Sagasti after violent protests
US drugmaker Moderna says its COVID-19 vaccine is 94.5% effective in early data
Astronauts on board the International Space Station forced to take shelter as Russian weapons test creates 1,500 debris field
Guanyu Zhou confirmed as China's first F1 driver, racing for Alfa Romeo in 2022
Men's roller derby team settles dispute with MLB baseball team, allowing both to use Cleveland Guardians name
Democratic congresswoman Karen Bass is the first woman elected mayor of the city of Los Angeles, defeating Rick Caruso
NASA's Artemis I mission carrying the uncrewed Orion spacecraft on a test mission around the Moon and back launches from Florida
U.S. Food and Drug Administration gives first safety approval on lab-grown meat, chicken grown from animal cells made by Upside Foods
Daryl Hall files a lawsuit and a request for a restraining order (later granted) against his music partner John Oates
MLB owners unanimously approve Oakland A's proposed move from California to Las Vegas, Nevada; it will become the franchise's 4th home since being established in Philadelphia in 1901
GeneralTiberius' (later Emperor) triumphant procession through Rome after siege of Germany
Battle of Lützen: significant battle of Thirty Years' War - Swedish and Saxon forces defeat the Holy Roman Empire, at cost of the death of Swedish King Gustavus Adolphus
French state funeral for Henrietta Maria, princess of France, widow of English KingCharles I, at St Denis with famous oration by BishopJacques-Bénigne Bossuet
English journalistJohn Wilkesinjured in a duel
"Extracts from Letters to Henslow", a collection of letters written byCharles Darwinduring his voyage on the Beagle, is read to the Cambridge Philosophical Society and later published as a pamphlet
Russian court sentencesFyodor Dostoevskyto death for anti-government activities linked to a radical intellectual group; his sentence is later commuted to hard labor
Aleksandr Ostrovsky's play "Groza" (The Storm) premieres in Moscow
Arturo Toscaninibegins conducting NY's Metropolitan Opera
PopeBenedict XVcalls for peace
PopePius XIcalls on Belgian people to unite
Brazilian President Getulio Vargas declares himself dictator
US PresidentFranklin Rooseveltestablishes diplomatic relations with USSR
Richard RodgersandLorenz Hart's musical "Jumbo" premieres in NYC
LSD (Lysergic acid diethylamide) is first synthesized by Swiss chemist Dr.Albert Hofmannat the Sandoz Laboratories in Basel, Switzerland
Al Caponefreed from Alcatraz jail
Memorandum signed by US PresidentHarry Truman, British PMClement Attlee, and Canadian PMMackenzie Kingto continue atomic energy cooperation
American murderer and bodysnatcherEd Geinkills his last victim
Boson Celtics' centerBill Russellsets NBA record of 49 rebounds as Boston beats Philadelphia Warriors, 111-89 at Boston Gardens
US PresidentJFKdecides to increase military aid to South Vietnam without committing US combat troops
SF Warriors centerWilt Chamberlainscores 73 points in 127-111 win over NY Knicks at Madison Square Garden
Pittsburgh Pirates outfielderRoberto Clementeis named NL MVP
US PresidentRichard Nixonbecomes first president to attend a season NFL game while in office: the Dallas Cowboys visit the Washington Redskins, winning 41-28
Apple Records releasesJohn Lennon's fourth studio album, "Mind Games" in UK
SingerDavid Bowieperforms on TV's "Midnight Special", set includes duet with Marianne Faithfull coveringSonny & Cher's hit "I Got You, Babe"
ABBAdeparts Sweden for their first tour of Europe - their first tour outside of their home country
Rick Barry(San Francisco), ends then longest NBA free throw streak of 60
Rod Carewwins AL MVP award
Paul McCartneyreleases "Wonderful Christmas"
President Reagandecides on a covert plan to block the Cuban aid to Nicaragua and El Salvador
Tom Stoppard's play "The Real Thing" premieres in London starring Felicity Kendal and Roger Rees
President Reaganarrives in Geneva for a summit with Soviet leaderMikhail Gorbachev
Dennis Potter's TV drama "The Singing Detective", starringMichael Gambon, premieres on the BBC in the UK
José Cansecois the first unanimous AL MVP sinceReggie Jackson
Pakistan'sBenazir Bhutto's PPP wins 1st free Pakistani elections in 11 years
President Reaganand the First Lady participate in the official state arrival ceremony, meetings and a state dinner withMargaret Thatcher
Robin GivenssuesMike Tysonfor $125 million for libel
South African PresidentF. W. de Klerkannounces scrapping of Separate Amenities Act
Former Panamanian dictatorManuel Noriegaclaims the US denied him a fair trial
Russian PresidentBoris YeltsinshutsLeninmuseum
Queen Elizabeth theQueen Motherundergoes hip surgery
American tennis greatPete Sampraswins back-to-back ATP Tour World Championship titles with a 6–3, 6–2, 6–2 over Russian Yevgeny Kafelnikov in Hanover, Germany
Monica Lewinskysigns a deal for the North American rights to a book about her affair with USPresident Clinton
"The Chronic 2001" second studio album by rapperDr. Dreis released
Bill Clintonbecomes the first U.S. President to visit Vietnam since the end of the Vietnam War

"Harry Potterand the Sorcerer's Stone", 1st film adaptation of the book series byJ. K. RowlingstarringDaniel Radcliffe, premieres in US (Titled "Harry Potter and the Philosopher's's Stone" in some markets)
Iraqi PresidentSaddam Husseinsays that he had to accept UN Resolution 1441 because the United States and Israel had shown their "claws and teeth" and declared unitlateral war on the Iraqi people
16-year-oldLionel Messimakes his official debut for FC Barcelona when he comes on as a substitute in a friendly against Porto
Roger Federerof Switzerland wins his first of 6 season-ending Tennis Masters Cup titles with a 6–3, 6–0, 6–4 victory over AmericanAndre Agassiin the final in Houston, Texas
CBS television broadcasts ”I Walk the Line: A Night ForJohnny Cash” featuring concert performances by Dwight Yoakam,Martina McBride,Alison Krauss,U2,Norah Jones,Foo Fighters,Sheryl Crow, and others
Gilberto Rodríguez Orejuelapleads guilty to money laundering, sentenced to additional 87 month concurrent term
Novak Đokovićof Serbia wins his first career season-ending Tennis Masters Cup title beating Russian Nikolay Davydenko 6-1, 7-5 in the final in Shanghai, China
"TheTwilight Saga: New Moon", based on the book byStephenie Meyer, directed by Chris Weitz, starringKristen StewartandRobert Pattinson, premieres in Los Angeles
Engagement announced between BritishPrince WilliamandKate Middletonat Clarence House, London
In University Park, Texas, the groundbreaking ceremony for theGeorge W. BushPresidential Center takes place
Irish rockerBonocrashes his bicycle and suffers arm, hand and eye socket injuries in Central Park, NYC
French PresidentFrançois Hollandedeclares the country at war with ISIS in an address to parliament
CIA concludes Saudi Crown PrinceMohammed bin Salmanordered the killing of journalistJamal Khashoggi
Frida Kahlo's self-portrait "Diego y yo" (1949) sells for $34.9m - record price at for a Latin American work at auction
US Republican party regains control of the House of Representatives by a narrow margin, withKevin McCarthyas Leader of the House
Revival of John Du Prez and Eric Idle's musical "Spamalot", based on the film "Monty Pythonand the Holy Grail," opens at the St. James Theatre, NYC
Spanish conquistadorFrancisco Pizarrocaptures Inca Emperor Atahualpa after a surprise ambush at Cajamarca in the Peruvian Andes
TsarIvan the Terribleattacks his son and heir, Ivan Ivanovich, with a scepter after an argument leading to the latter's death three days later
US diplomat John Lloyd Stephens and British artistFrederick Catherwoodfirst reach the ruins of the Maya city of Copan (modern Honduras)
Ottoman Caliph, Sultan Mehmed VI asks the British army for help
BBC’s 1st pop music show, the "Six-Five Special", is broadcast from the tiny 2i’s Coffee Bar in London
US Marshals escort four six-year-old African American girls to previously all-white public schools in New Orleans in response to death threats against the girls and race riots
The first case of SARS is recorded in Foshan City, Guangdong Province, China, though is not identified until much later. First patient is thought to be a farmer in the city.
Leonardo Loredan, Venetian nobleman, statesman and Doge of Venice (1501-21), born in Venice (d. 1521)
Jeanne d'Albret, Queen of Navarre (1555-72), mother of French King Henry IV, born in the royal palace of Saint-Germain-en-Laye, France (d. 1572)
Paul Sartorius, German composer, born in the Free Imperial City of Nuremberg, Holy Roman Empire (d. 1609)
Joost van den Vondel, Dutch poet and dramatist (Jephtha), often regarded as the greatest writer in Dutch, born in Cologne, Germany (d. 1679)
Augustyn Kordecki, Polish prior, born in Iwanowice, Kalisz, Poland (d. 1673)
Henrietta Maria, French-born daughter of Henry IV of France and Queen Consort of England, born at the Palais du Louvre, Paris (d. 1669)
Guillaume Dumanoir, French composer, born in Paris (d. 1697)

Étienne Pivert de Senancour, French author (Obermann), born in Paris (d. 1846)

Elliott Warren Rice, American Brevet Major General (Union Army), born in Allegheny, Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania (d. 1887)
Eugenio Beltrami, Italian mathematician (non-Euclidean geometry), born in Cremona, Lombardy, Austrian Empire (d. 1900)

Edmund James Flynn, Canadian politician (10th Premier of Quebec), born in Percé, Canada (d. 1927)
Jules van den Heuvel, Belgian lawyer, politician (Minister of Justice, 1899-1907), and diplomat (Ambassador to the Vatican, 1915-18), born in Ghent, Belgium (d. 1926)
Edmund Schücker, Austrian composer, born in Vienna (d. 1911)
Václav Suk, Czech-born Russian composer and violinist, born in Kladno, Bohemia (d. 1933)
Charles Turner, Australian cricket fast bowler (17 Tests; 101 wickets @ 16.53; fastest to 50 Test wickets; NSWCA), born in Bathurst, Australia (d. 1944)
Halliwell Hobbes, British actor (Gaslight, Dr Jekyll & Mr Hyde), born in Stratford-on-Avon, Warwickshire, England (d. 1962)
Thomas Ince, American actor and silent film director who pioneered the Western genre, born in Newport, Rhode Island (d. 1924)

Joel H. Hildebrand, American chemist and educator (research led to new treatments for divers with the 'bends' through the use of helium and oxygen breathing mixtures), born in Camden, New Jersey (d. 1983)
Eugeen Van de Velde, Flemish musicologist, choir director, and composer (In Tempore Belli), born in Belgium (d. 1958)

Lawrence Tibbett, American baritone (Metropolitan 1923-50), born in Bakersfield, California (d. 1960)

Choudhary Rehmat Ali, Pakistani nationalist (one of the earliest proponents of the creation of the state of Pakistan and recognized as the creator of the name “Pakistan” for a separate Muslim, homeland in South Asia and is known as the founder of the Pakistan National Movement), born in Garhshankar, Punjab, British India (d. 1951)

Sœur Emmanuelle [Madeleine Cinquin], Belgian-French and honorary Egyptian religious sister (Roman Catholic), human rights worker, and writer (Un pauvre a crié, le Seigneur l'écoute), born in Brussels, Belgium (d. 2008)

Betty Hicks, American golfer, author (US Open runner-up 1948, 54), born in Long Beach, California (d. 2011)

Carlos Camacho, American dentist, and politician (1st elected Governor of Guam, 1971-75), born in Hagåtña, Guam (d. 1979)

(William) "Clu" Gulager, American actor (Virginian; The Tall Man; The Last Picture Show), born in Holdenville, Oklahoma (d. 2022)
Renate Rubinstein, German-Dutch author and columnist (Tamar), born in Berlin (d. 1990)

Guy Stockwell, American stage and screen actor (Adventures in Paradise - "Chris"; Beau Geste (1966)), born in New York City (d. 2002)

Mike Giddings, American football coach and scout (HC University of Utah, Denver Broncos director pro scouting; pioneer independent scouting & NFL analytics), born in Newport Beach, California (d. 2023)
